items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения» and keeps the clipped parallelism «У Шпака — …, у посла — …» that drives the comedic ‘connecting-the-dots’ rhythm. Also matches the reference’s choice to avoid over-explaining the joke.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It keeps the repetitive, banal politeness that is the core of the joke (Soviet-era social cliché intruding into a royal setting), and the rhythm of the repeated self-identification “the Tsar” is preserved.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The imperative ‘I demand’ correctly conveys the sudden authoritarian persona and keeps the punchy, quotable cadence.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It preserves the archaic/high-register melancholy and the comic mismatch with the modern domestic situation; ‘old woman’ keeps the insult blunt and era-marked.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It keeps ‘demons’ as the agent and preserves the panicked, incantatory repetition that sells the misunderstanding of technology as sorcery.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It retains the elevated, religious proclamation tone and the comic attribution of a mechanical event to divine intervention.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It captures the MC/announcer vibe and the permissive ‘everyone join in’ function, keeping the comedic ‘party command’ energy.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It keeps the breezy self-satisfaction and the idiomatic feel (a burglar’s cheerful appraisal), matching the intended comic beat.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread ‘caviar’ in English, preserving the joke’s inversion (luxury caviar vs humble Soviet ‘eggplant’ presented as exotic).

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It preserves the comic inventory cadence, the self-important ‘domestic’ qualifier, and the trailing ‘Three’ that signals the escalating/exaggerated tally.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It keeps the calm, sing-song resignation and the shared ‘we’re all going to the psych ward’ punchline without overplaying it.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
